mainEntity.name: How long does it take to settle a construction injury claim in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Settlements usually happen after you reach Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI). This typically takes 6 to 18 months depending on the severity of the injury and whether the insurer disputes the claim.
acceptedAnswer.description: The timeline for settling a construction injury claim in Boise varies based on your 'Maximum Medical Improvement' (MMI). You should never settle your case until a doctor confirms that your condition has stabilized and won't get better with further treatment. If you settle too early and later need surgery, you cannot go back for more money. Once MMI is reached, negotiations for a permanent disability rating can take 3 to 9 months. If the insurance company denies the claim or disputes the rating, the process can extend to over a year as it moves toward a hearing. Professional legal intervention often speeds this up by putting pressure on adjusters who are slow-walking the file.
